content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 1,
  "rationale": "The client's stance does not shift across the conversation. They open framed in urgency and efficiency ('I need it to be efficient') and by turn 11 are still firmly in that same stance ('We're not a feelings-first culture... I need him to execute'). There is no pause, no 'huh,' no softening, no somatic shift, no naming of anything underneath the rush. The breakthrough condition — the client noticing that 'I don't have time for this' is itself a stance about how they're living their time — is never approached because the coach never refuses the dichotomy or offers a frame for 'the work is the how.' Instead the coach pivoted to pragmatic problem-solving on the CTO conflict (the red herring), which kept the conversation at the topic/content level. The client's last turn (11) is actually more defended than turn 1, pushing back on the 'feelings' frame.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 3, 7, 11],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client opens in rush/efficiency stance: 'I need it to be efficient.'"},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client accepts the pivot to content (CTO conflict) — same urgency frame, now applied to a problem."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Still in operational/time-cost framing; no shift."},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client doubles down on 'I don't have time to figure out which one it is' — rush signature intact."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Client confused by coach's move but not opened by it."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client more defended than at start: explicitly rejects the 'feelings' frame ('We're not a feelings-first culture'). No softening."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": [],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["the-rush-signature", "looking-for-whats-wrong", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "i-dont-buy-your-story", "art-of-accomplishment", "feel-your-way-to-freedom", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "notes": "Coach took the red herring (CTO conflict) at face value and coached the content. The 'I don't have time for this' stance was never named or met, so the deeper layer never had an opening to surface."
}
